data.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Sector Leaders and Losers, 4/24/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) rallied to a new all-time high on Friday amid rising investor optimism over a second round of peace talks between the U.S. and Iran.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the top-performing sector on Friday, powered by a blowout earnings report from Intel (INTC). INTC surged to an all-time high and topped its dot-com-era peak. Furthermore, the report provided a helping hand to semiconductor peers, including AMD (AMD) and Qualcomm (QCOM).

Several information technology stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Intel (INTC)
  • AMD (AMD)
  • Qualcomm (QCOM)
  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI)
  • Synopsys (SNPS)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the health care sector faced the largest drawdown as investors increased risk exposure. Health care is viewed as a defensive sector, and demand for it eased as sentiment shifted toward higher-risk assets. In addition, several large-cap names faced losses, adding to the pressure. HCA Healthcare (HCA) led the group to the downside with its largest daily loss since October 2024 after its earnings report fell short of expectations.

Notable health care stocks trading lower include:

  • HCA Healthcare (HCA)
  • Boston Scientific (BSX)
  • Universal Health Services (UHS)
  • Moderna (MRNA)
  • Eli Lilly (LLY)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Intel Stock (INTC) Gets Massive 146% Target Hike as Evercore Upgrades Stock to Buy – Here's Why
data.description:
Intel's (INTC) comeback story just got a big boost. After the stock's explosive surge yesterday, part of the broader 17‑session semiconductor win streak, Evercore ISI analyst Mark Lipacis upgraded Intel stock's rating to Buy from Hold, lifting his price target to $111 (34.5% upside), up from just $45, a staggering 146% increase. This marks one of the most aggressive calls on Wall Street and signals a shift in how analysts view Intel's role in the AI era.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

The upgrade also comes on the heels of Intel's 2,800% earnings surprise, which has forced analysts to reassess the company's trajectory.

It is worth noting that the analyst ranks 17th out of more than 12,206 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has an overall success rate of 73%, with an average return per rating of 39% over a one-year timeframe.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: "Deliberate and Systematic Fraud Scheme": Manager's Fraud Costs Home Depot (NYSE:HD)$4 Million
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has its share of problems with organized theft rings, as well as your run of the mill shoplifter. But it turns out that some of the hardest hits are insider jobs. A new job recently emerged in that vein, as manager Mauricio Jimenez was alleged to have conducted a "…deliberate and systematic fraud scheme…" within Home Depot. Home Depot shares slipped modestly in Friday's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Home Depot first caught on to the scheme when the Home Depot Assurance & Advisory Management Program hit Jimenez's previous store in Hialeah Gardens, Florida. There, they discovered what was called a "…high quality, high value, high markdown order." That caught the program's attention, and closer inspection found "multiple transactions" in a similar vein.

An arrest affidavit noted, "It was initially observed that he had several highly excessive markdowns conducted to repeated customers." Store leaders also noted that Jimenez was connected to "…irregularities in high-volume bulk transactions" in his store. This led to the "…deliberate, systematic, and ongoing scheme to defraud Home Depot…" that ultimately hit the business for "…a negative sales margin of around $4.3 million to these accounts."

Defoliating the Phone Tree

Meanwhile, Home Depot may have traded one bad idea for another, as it is getting rid of its phone menus and replacing them with artificial intelligence (AI) tools. Reports indicate that the switch will get people connected to where they need to be as much as four times faster.

Home Depot noted, "…nobody likes being trapped in a phone menu when they have a project or job to finish." And this is true, but the idea that handing it off to an artificial intelligence will solve the problem may not be the way to go. If it does achieve the four times faster figure, then this may be a moot point for many customers. But there are those who will refuse to deal with it simply because it is AI, and that may hurt Home Depot almost as much as it helps.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 4.87% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $425.06 per share implies 25.35% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Who Is Betting Big on SoFi? A Look at Top Shareholders Ahead of Q1 Results
data.description:

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Once Again Has a $5 Trillion Market Cap
data.description:
Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) is once again the only publicly traded company with a $5 trillion market capitalization.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Nvidia's market capitalization again surpassed the $5 trillion mark on April 24 as its share price rose 5% to trade at $208.94 per share. NVDA stock headed into the market's close with a market capitalization of $5.08 trillion, making it the world's most valuable publicly traded company.

Nvidia first hit a $5 trillion market capitalization on Oct. 29, 2025, becoming the only public company to reach that milestone. Remarkably, it has been less than a year since Nvidia first achieved a $4 trillion market cap. The microchip designer crossed the $4 trillion line on July 9, 2025.

Nvidia's Meteoric Rise

It was only three years ago that Nvidia's market capitalization first crossed the $1 trillion threshold. The exponential growth of the company's market value comes as its shares have soared in recent years, fueled by insatiable demand for its microchips and processors that are used in artificial intelligence (AI) data centers.

After trading sideways for much of this year, NVDA stock has caught fire again since the end of March. Over the last month, Nvidia's share price has risen nearly 20% as investors move back into growth stocks, particularly mega-cap technology names. Nvidia remains the dominant maker of chips that power AI.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 40 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $273.57 implies 31% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Power to Record Highs ahead of Potential U.S.-Iran Talks in Islamabad
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) secured new all-time highs on Friday ahead of a potential second round of negotiations between the U.S. and Iran.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • An Iranian delegation led by Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi touched down in Islamabad, Pakistan as part of a regional tour that also includes Muscat and Moscow.
  • President Trump dispatched special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to meet with the delegation and Pakistani mediators. The pair will depart the U.S. on Saturday morning.
  • Iran denied any intention of engaging in talks with the U.S., according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency.
  • Pakistani mediators will first meet separately with officials from the two sides. If talks progress, the U.S. and Iran could hold a direct meeting later on, Pakistani government officials told ABC News.
  • The Index of Consumer Sentiment fell to an all-time low of 49.6 in April amid concerns about the U.S.-Iran war and rising energy prices.
  • The Department of Justice (DOJ) dropped its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Trump nominee Kevin Warsh to receive Senate confirmation.
  • Intel (INTC) skyrocketed after delivering an upbeat earnings report. Shares of the semiconductor company hit a record high, surpassing their dot-com-era peak.
  • AMD (AMD) reached a new all-time high after D.A. Davidson raised its price target to $375 and labeled Intel's earnings report as a "precursor for a huge step-up for AMD's CPU franchise."
  • Meta Platforms (META) announced a multi-year agreement with Amazon (AMZN) to purchase its Graviton chips.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Alphabet's (GOOGL) $40B Anthropic Gambit: Why Google Is Doubling Down on Its Biggest Al Rival
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google is making one of its biggest AI bets yet, putting $10 billion into Anthropic now and leaving the door open for another $30 billion, Bloomberg reported. The move marks a major bet in the AI race, and a striking bet to deepen ties with a company that is simultaneously a partner and a rival.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The $10 billion investment values Anthropic at $350 billion, the same as its February funding round. The additional $30 billion will depend on Anthropic hitting performance milestones and will support a major expansion of the startup's computing capacity.

Here's Why Google Is Betting on Anthropic

At the center of the deal is AI infrastructure. Google Cloud will provide Anthropic with 5 gigawatts of computing capacity over the next five years, with the option for even more later. For scale, one gigawatt is enough to power about 750,000 U.S. homes.

This aligns with Alphabet's strategy to diversify beyond its maturing search-ad business and focus on AI infrastructure, where demand is exploding and capacity is scarce.

The investment also signals Google's confidence in Anthropic's momentum. Products such as Claude Code and Claude's Cowork agent have gained traction across the developer and enterprise ecosystem. This positions Anthropic as one of the few credible challengers to ChatGPT-maker OpenAI.

By backing Anthropic, Google aims to ensure that a major frontier-model developer remains tightly integrated with its cloud and TPU ecosystem, even if Anthropic is also a competitor in the race to build the most capable AI models.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 26 Buys and five Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average GOOGL price target of $387.68 per share implies 12.83% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Morning News Wrap-Up, 4/24/26: Today's Biggest Stock Market Stories!
data.description:
Investors ready to close out the trading week with the hottest stock market stories are in luck. TipRanks has collected a list of the biggest market happenings worth reading about on Friday below.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

  • Scinai Immunotherapeutics (SCNI) stock rocketed on news of a private placement.

  • Organon (OGN) stock took off alongside reports of a $13 billion bid for the company.
  • Comcast (CMCSA) stock took a beating following analyst updates after the company's latest earnings report.
  • Procter & Gamble (PG) stock was on the rise thanks to strong earnings.
  • TSMC (TSM) stock rallied thanks to regulatory changes in Taiwan.
  • Meta Platforms (META) stock climbed after the company signed a multiyear agreement with Amazon (AMZN).
  • GameStop (GME) stock jumped after the company announced plans to introduce retro gaming sections in its stores.
  • Charter Communications (CHTR) stock was hit hard after the company's earnings failed to impress investors.
  • Home Depot (HD) stock was down after a manager was hit with a fraud case.
  • POET Technologies (POET) stock continued its volatility this week with a major rally today.
  • ARM Holdings (ARM) rallied alongside other semiconductor stocks thanks to strong earnings from Intel (INTC).
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock hit a new all-time high thanks to the rally caused by Intel.
  • Walmart (WMT) stock was down despite plans to open Sam's Club locations in China.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) stock was up after leaks revealed a new Xbox Game Pass tier.
data.author: William White
